target.title: fix(ui): preserve live tool stream order
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes live WebChat ordering when a tool event timestamp is older than the assistant stream segment that introduced that tool call.
- Threads optional `toolCallId` metadata through chat stream segment types so the renderer can keep the matching tool card after its causal assistant text while preserving ordinary timestamp ordering.
- Adds regression coverage for both the delayed/live timestamp race and same-millisecond multi-segment ordering behind #92122.

Fixes #92122

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: WebChat live streaming can render a tool card before the assistant text that triggered it when the tool message timestamp sorts earlier than the stream segment.

Real environment tested: Local Control UI browser E2E against the mocked Gateway in the OpenClaw checkout on Node 22 via repo test wrappers.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts` with a case that sends a live assistant delta, then a tool-start event with an older timestamp, and verifies the browser DOM order.

Evidence after fix: The browser E2E asserted the visible order is `["assistant stream", "tool card"]`. The screenshot is uploaded as a direct PNG artifact here:

![WebChat live tool ordering proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Pick-cat/openclaw/proof/pr-93184-webchat-order/webchat-live-tool-order.png)

- Direct PNG: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Pick-cat/openclaw/proof/pr-93184-webchat-order/webchat-live-tool-order.png
- Artifact branch: https://github.com/Pick-cat/openclaw/tree/proof/pr-93184-webchat-order
- Screenshot SHA-256: `36736faaae68a850d3b43662fb0305222f34c85645435077c0fdda55f683e65b`

Observed result after fix: The browser E2E passed, and the uploaded screenshot shows `I will inspect the file.` above the matching tool card.

What was not tested: I did not run a real remote Gateway. The browser proof is local and mocked, but it exercises the actual Control UI rendering path and includes an inspectable direct PNG artifact.
target.author: pick-cat
